channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Zulu king calls for orderly migration in Africa during Zimbabwe visit
item.description: Zulu king calls for orderly migration in Africa during Zimbabwe visit — via Daily Dispatch #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Daily Dispatch

item.title: Police say Mark Tshuma bought gun to take his own life as family’s cause of death revealed @nehandaradio
item.description: Police in South Africa allege Mark Tshuma bought an illegal firearm intending to end his own life, while post-mortem examinations have revealed how his wife and two daughters died. The post Police say Mark Tshuma bought gun to take his own life as family’s cause of death revealed appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: ZACC chairperson Michael Reza’s wife Chipo dies after collapsing at home @nehandaradio
item.description: Chipo Reza, the wife of Zimbabwe Anti-Corruption Commission chairperson Michael Reza, has died after collapsing at her Harare home on Sunday. The post ZACC chairperson Michael Reza’s wife Chipo dies after collapsing at home appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Zimbabweans prioritize funeral cover over health insurance @bulawayo24
item.description: As Zimbabwe grapples with shrinking donor support for healthcare, experts have warned that a deeply rooted cultural paradox is undermining efforts to build a sustainable health system: millions of Zim... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Zimbabwean banker was preparing for divorce before UK family tragedy @bulawayo24
item.description: New details have emerged suggesting that Zimbabwean banker Nothabo Zandile Tshuma was preparing to end her marriage and begin a new life with her two daughters before she and the children were found d... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Zimbabwe govt to continue reviewing taxes, levies @bulawayo24
item.description: Government will continue reviewing taxes and levies to ensure they reflect prevailing economic conditions, improve the ease of doing business and support Zimbabwe’s goal of attaining upper-middle-inco... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Backlash over Mnangagwa’s CAB 3 consensus claim @bulawayo24 @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s assertion that the Constitution of Zimbabwe Amendment No. 3 Act was the product of "collective wisdom" has triggered renewed political debate, with some Zanu-PF insiders... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Honda Fits Under Siege: The Secret Syndicate Behind Zimbabwe’s Rising Car Thefts
item.description: Harare – A shadow of fear has fallen over Zimbabwean motorists, particularly those who own the ubiquitous Honda Fit. What was once a symbol of affordable mobility has now become a beacon for a sophisticated criminal syndicate, turning everyday commutes into potential nightmares. Recent events have brought this escalating crisis into sharp focus, with police [...] The post Honda Fits Under Siege: The Secret Syndicate Behind Zimbabwe’s Rising Car Thefts first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Mapfumo confirms Zimbabwe return for Chivayo’s US$1 million performances @bulawayo24 @wicknellchivayo
item.description: CHIMURENGA music legend Thomas "Mukanya" Mapfumo has confirmed that he will return to Zimbabwe for two high-profile performances after accepting businessman Wicknell Chivayo’s invitation to perform at... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: It’s all work – Chimurenga music legend Mapfumo accepts Chivayo’s US$1 million offer @wicknellchivayo
item.description: CHIMURENGA music legend Thomas Mapfumo has confirmed he will return to Zimbabwe from his United States base to perform at controversial businessman Wicknell Chivayo’s birthday celebration and musician Jah Prayzah’s wedding, ending weeks of speculation over whether he would accept the US$1 million offer. The announcement follows Chivayo’s offer to pay the veteran musician US$500,000 [...] The post It’s all work – Chimurenga music legend Mapfumo accepts Chivayo’s US$1 million offer appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Mark Tshuma appears in South African court as UK seeks extradition over family murders @nehandaradio
item.description: Mark Tshuma has appeared in a Johannesburg court after his arrest last week, with South African authorities confirming the UK has submitted a provisional extradition request over the deaths of his wife and two daughters. The post Mark Tshuma appears in South African court as UK seeks extradition over family murders appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Professor Herbert Chinyanga Declared National Hero After Distinguished Medical Career
item.description: President Mnangagwa Confers National Hero Status On Professor Herbert Chinyanga Zimbabwe is mourning one of its most respected medical pioneers after President Emmerson Mnangagwa conferred National Hero status on Professor Herbert Mapfumo Chinyanga. The renowned physician, anaesthetist and educator died on 2 July 2026 at the age of 87 while receiving treatment in South Africa. [...] The post Professor Herbert Chinyanga Declared National Hero After Distinguished Medical Career appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma’s Extradition and Firearm Cases Postponed in Johannesburg Court
item.description: Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma’s Extradition and Firearm Cases Postponed in Johannesburg Court Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma, has been remanded in custody after making his first appearance in the Johannesburg Magistrate’s Court on charges linked to the alleged unlawful possession of a firearm. The 45-year-old appeared in court on Monday following his arrest in Kensington, Johannesburg, during a [...] The post Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma’s Extradition and Firearm Cases Postponed in Johannesburg Court appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Goodbye all illegal foreigners: South African Government introduces On-Street handheld biometric devices
item.description: Is the age of the physical passport finally coming to an end in South Africa? Or is this the beginning of a digital dragnet that no one can escape? In this documentary, we delve into a massive shift in how South Africa handles its borders and the people within them. For years, the debate over [...] The post Goodbye all illegal foreigners: South African Government introduces On-Street handheld biometric devices first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: SA Court Refuses Bail For Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma As UK Extradition Battle Begins
item.description: Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma Denied Bail In SA On Firearms Charges Zimbabwean national Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma has been denied bail in South Africa after appearing in the Johannesburg Magistrate’s Court on firearms-related charges, while also facing extradition proceedings linked to the alleged murders of his wife and two children in the United Kingdom. The matter was [...] The post SA Court Refuses Bail For Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma As UK Extradition Battle Begins appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: After the Killing Comes the Blame: Why Dead Women Are Still Put on Trial @263Chat
item.description: The most troubling aspect of public discussion surrounding the Tshuma case has been the extent to which allegations of infidelity have come to dominate the narrative despite their complete irrelevance to the moral and legal questions at the centre of the tragedy, The post After the Killing Comes the Blame: Why Dead Women Are Still Put on Trial appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat
